description_long: This object is a single orange flower with multiple elongated petals radiating outward from a central point. The petals are slightly curved and overlap each other, creating a layered appearance. The flower is attached to a slender green stem, which is visible beneath the petals. The surface beneath the flower appears to be a white, slightly textured background with green markings, possibly writing or drawing. The flower's coloration transitions from a deeper orange at the base of the petals to a lighter, almost yellowish hue at the tips. No leaves are visible near the flower, and the overall structure suggests it is a freshly picked or artificial flower placed on a flat surface.
description: An orange flower with layered, elongated petals radiating from a central point and attached to a green stem, resting on a white surface with green markings.
description_view_0: The flower is seen from above, showing the full arrangement of orange petals and the green stem, with green markings visible on the white background.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the thickness of the flower and the curvature of the petals, with the stem extending backward.
description_view_2: The underside of the flower is visible, highlighting the base of the petals and the attachment to the stem, with less detail of the petal faces.
description_view_3: A narrow side profile emphasizes the thinness of the flower and the layering of the petals, with the stem barely visible.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, radiating symmetrically from the center of the flower. They overlap slightly at the base, forming a whorled pattern typical of many ornamental flowers.
